rating: 16+ — blood, death, romance, moral complexity. No outcome is protected.
tagline: Where every truth is a lie waiting to be remembered
overview: Arion World is a vast pre-electricity world where magic is used like technology — practically, daily, without being understood. The Founding Age left ruins so advanced that modern civilisation built on top of them without knowing what they are. The world appears small at first. It is not. New continents, civilisations, and power scales keep being revealed as the story grows. At the heart of everything lies the Arion Fracture — a shattering of time itself whose consequences are still unfolding 2847 years later.

world_setting.technology_level: Pre-electricity. Candles, forges, horses, ships. Manual craftsmanship. No engines, no grid, no machines — except for Founding Age remnants that still function and that nobody understands.
world_setting.magic_in_daily_life: Magic is used the way modern people use phones — practically and constantly, with almost no understanding of how it works. A baker's bread always rises. A sailor feels storms before they form. A blacksmith's metal folds better under his hands than any other smith's. Nobody calls this magic. It is simply life. The people who study and understand the underlying mechanics become vastly more capable — but most people live and die as users, never engineers.

founding_age_remnants.description: The Founding Age civilisation left structures and mechanisms 2847 years ago that modern people have absorbed into daily life without understanding. These remnants are everywhere in Caelum City and beyond.
examples: Walls built from a stone nobody can quarry or replicate — after 2847 years they have not cracked
A gate in the city centre that opens at dawn and closes at dusk on its own — market stalls route around it, people leave small offerings, it has a shrine now
A well in the outer district that has never run dry including during the three-year drought sixty years ago — people call it god-touched
Script carved into every foundation stone in the city — scholars declared it decorative because they cannot read it
Roads so perfectly level that water runs off them in straight lines — laid before anyone alive was born, still used daily
Dead zones scattered across the known world where Resonance does not work — avoided, considered cursed, never explained
founding_age_remnants.the_undercity: Directly beneath Caelum City lies the intact original Founding Age city — sealed, forgotten, and containing archives that would rewrite everything the Guild teaches. The door in the deepest level is written in the same script as the foundation stones. Nobody has opened it.

world_setting.map_philosophy: The world expands as the story earns it. Season 1 takes place in and around Caelum City — it feels like the complete world. It is not. Beyond the Fractured Wastes is an entire civilisation that has been watching Caelum for centuries. Beyond that are continents. The audience will not see the full map for years — each expansion must be earned by the story reaching it naturally.

skill_system.name: The Open Path
skill_system.core_philosophy: There are no locked classes, no birthright restrictions, no skill trees with gates. Anyone can learn anything. The only limits are time, knowledge, and understanding. A human lifespan is not long enough to master everything — so every person is constantly choosing what to spend their years on. Those choices define them.
skill_system.the_phone_truth: Magic works the way phones work. Everyone can use it. Almost nobody understands it. The people who figure out how it actually works — even partially — become exponentially more capable than those who only know how to press the buttons. The Founding Age people understood the source code. Modern users are tapping screens without knowing what a processor is.
skill_system.knowledge_scarcity: There are no complete guides. No manuals. No schools that teach everything. Knowledge is fragmented — passed person to person with pieces missing at every step. If a master dies without teaching their technique, that technique may be gone forever. Finding someone who knows a rare method and convincing them to teach you is worth more than a year of solo training. This is why other races matter — they carry knowledge humans lost or never had.

raw_power_vs_precision.description: Raw power used bluntly always loses to precision used intelligently — eventually. This is the fundamental truth most powerful people learn too late.
raw_power_vs_precision.example_water_magic: A skilled water mage throwing walls of water looks powerful. The person who spent ten years understanding pressure, velocity, and surface tension fires a sphere the size of a coin at speeds that punch through castle walls. Less energy. More destruction. Invisible until it hits. The second person studied what the first person performed.
raw_power_vs_precision.example_clone_technique: A fighter who learns to create a physical clone uses it in battle — the clone takes hits and dies. A thinker who learns the same technique uses the clone to study simultaneously: one body learning swordsmanship while the other reads ancient texts. If they find a way to recover what the clone learned before it dissolves, one lifetime becomes two. The skill is identical. The application determines everything.
raw_power_vs_precision.the_guild_problem: The Resonant Guild teaches the obvious application of every skill. Always. The obvious application is controllable, predictable, taxable. The moment someone finds a non-obvious application — something the Guild did not teach and did not anticipate — they become unpredictable. The Guild calls this dangerous. What it actually is, is free.

branches.note: These are not classes you choose. They are how scholars attempt to organise what is actually a continuous field of knowledge. The boundaries between branches are not real — the most powerful techniques always cross them.
branches.Body Arts: Physical conditioning, combat technique, speed, endurance, pain tolerance, muscle memory. No Resonance required. A warrior who has spent thirty years on Body Arts alone is genuinely dangerous to careless Resonants. Physical mastery can be combined with any other branch — a fighter who understands both Body Arts and Resonance mechanics simultaneously moves in ways that look impossible.
branches.Resonance Arts: The study and application of Luminal Resonance — the energy that permeates all living things and the world itself. Most Resonants learn surface techniques. The deep mechanics — why Resonance works, what it connects to, what it costs — are either unknown, suppressed by the Guild, or preserved only in Founding Age archives.
branches.Essence Arts: Manipulation of living things — healing, body modification, growth, decay. Considered sacred by some cultures, forbidden by others. The Guild teaches only basic healing. The full depth of Essence Arts includes things the Guild has classified as illegal without public explanation.
branches.Perception Arts: Sensing, reading, tracking, anticipating. Knowing what is about to happen a half-second before it does. Reading a person's intent from their breathing. Feeling a lie before it finishes. At deep levels, Perception Arts begin to overlap with Resonance in ways that make the boundary meaningless.
branches.Craft Arts: Making things — weapons, tools, structures, mechanisms. At surface level this is blacksmithing and carpentry. At deep levels, Craft Arts practitioners can embed techniques into objects, create tools that channel Resonance without the user understanding it, or reverse-engineer Founding Age technology piece by piece. The rarest and most hunted specialisation.
branches.Mind Arts: Memory, logic, strategy, mental resistance, knowledge organisation. The clone technique lives here. So does the ability to compartmentalise under torture, to hold complex plans without writing them down, to learn from observation rather than practice. The most underestimated branch — and the one the Veilkeepers quietly ensure gets the least teaching.
branches.The Unnamed Branch: Techniques that do not fit any category. Usually discovered by accident, or learned from races who do not share human frameworks for organising knowledge. The Guild has no curriculum for it. The Veilkeepers actively suppress documented instances of it. It keeps appearing anyway.

skill_system.other_races_and_knowledge: Different races have preserved different fragments of the complete picture. No race has everything. But each has things humans do not — techniques developed over generations, passed through cultures that share knowledge differently than humans do. A human who earns the trust of another race and learns their methods does not just gain new skills — they gain a new framework for understanding what they already knew. The world expanding on the map is also the knowledge map expanding.

The Founding Age.description: The age when the original Arion civilisation rose and fell. They were the engineers — they understood the source code of Luminal Resonance completely. What modern Resonants do in an hour, they did in a second. The Luminal Core was their greatest work and their undoing.
key_events: The Birth of the First Arion (Year 0)
The Great Betrayal — Varos kills Erian rather than watch them be consumed by the Core (Year 312)
The Arion Fracture — reality cracks into multiple parallel timelines as a consequence of the murder (Year 400)

The Shadow Centuries.description: The dark age. Civilisations rise and fall without understanding why time feels fractured. The Founding Age knowledge scatters, fragments, gets buried under new cities. Ancient orders form to hunt Core fragments — some to use them, some to prevent their use.

The Present Age.description: The primary timeline. Caelum City feels like the centre of the world. It is one city on one coast. The story begins here and grows outward.

The Echo Timeline.description: The timeline where the Great Betrayal was prevented. Developed completely differently — and is slowly being erased as timelines collapse toward a single point. People from here carry knowledge that does not exist in the main timeline.

The Far Future.description: Cryptic glimpses only. Technologically advanced, spiritually empty. The Luminal Core was found and misused. These scenes appear as warnings that the characters cannot yet interpret.

power_system.name: Luminal Resonance
power_system.description: The energy left behind by the Luminal Core's creation. It permeates all living things and the world itself. Most people use it unconsciously. Those who study it gain access to its deeper capabilities. Nobody in Year 2847 understands it fully — the Founding Age people did, and that knowledge is buried or suppressed.

tiers.name: Echo Sight
tiers.description: Conscious awareness of Resonance — perceiving emotional and event residue left on objects and places. Most people have this without knowing it.

tiers.name: Thread Weaving
tiers.description: Active manipulation of Resonance — shaping light, heat, force, perception. The level the Resonant Guild trains people to and considers 'mastery'.

tiers.name: Fracture Walking
tiers.description: Perceiving and briefly stepping between timelines. Considered mythical. The Guild has no record of it occurring in the last 500 years.

tiers.name: Core Binding
tiers.description: Theoretical. What happens when the Luminal Core is reassembled and fully understood. No living person has come close.

power_system.hidden_cost: Every use of Luminal Resonance at depth slowly anchors the user more completely to a single timeline — making them more real here, more ghost-like everywhere else. Resonants who push too far eventually cannot be perceived in any timeline but their own. Then they cannot be perceived at all. The Guild calls this 'Resonance Fade' and lists it as a known risk of overuse. They do not explain why it happens.
power_system.the_guild_ceiling: The Resonant Guild teaches Thread Weaving as the pinnacle of achievable power. This is not the ceiling. It is the ceiling they drew.

The Veilkeepers.role: Ancient secret society that knows the truth about the Arion Fracture. Their mission: prevent the Luminal Core from being reassembled at all costs.
The Veilkeepers.true_nature: They caused the Fracture deliberately to trap Varos inside it — and have spent 2847 years making sure nobody discovers this or undoes it
The Veilkeepers.genuine_belief: They believe reassembling the Core frees Varos, which ends the world. They may be right. They may be catastrophically wrong. They have never allowed the question to be tested.

The Resonant Guild.role: Controls access to Resonance education. Trains, licenses, and regulates Resonants across the known world.
The Resonant Guild.public_face: Academy and peacekeeping force — they maintain order, resolve Resonance-related incidents, protect citizens
The Resonant Guild.true_nature: Upper leadership are Veilkeeper instruments. The Guild exists to keep Resonance knowledge at Thread Weaving level and no deeper. Anyone developing beyond that ceiling gets flagged, redirected, or disappears.
The Resonant Guild.what_they_suppress: Any technique that approaches understanding WHY Resonance works. Any discovery relating to the Founding Age. Any skill that could theoretically interface with the Fracture itself.

The Chroniclers.role: A faction dedicated to collecting, cataloguing, and eventually publishing all knowledge — from every race, every tradition, every era. They believe knowledge hoarding is the root of all power imbalance.
The Chroniclers.public_face: Travelling scholars and archivists — harmless eccentrics who trade information
The Chroniclers.true_nature: The only faction actively working against knowledge scarcity. They have fragments of Founding Age technique that they are slowly piecing together. The Guild considers them a low-level threat. The Veilkeepers consider them an existential one.
The Chroniclers.what_they_want: A complete, accessible record of everything that can be known. No gates. No licenses. No secrets kept by institutions for power.

The Shattered.role: People who partially lost their timeline anchor — they exist across multiple timelines simultaneously.
The Shattered.true_nature: The only people who can naturally perceive the full structure of the Fracture. Their apparent instability is the sensation of existing in more than one version of reality at once.
The Shattered.secret: They are not broken. They are the most complete people alive.

The Unnamed Court.role: Unknown. Referenced in three surviving Founding Age texts in terms that suggest they predate even the Arion civilisation.
The Unnamed Court.true_nature: UNREVEALED — this is a long-game mystery. Do not resolve early.

Caelum City.description: The capital. Feels like the centre of the world in Season 1. Built on top of the original Founding Age city without the inhabitants knowing. The streets follow Founding Age road layouts. The walls are Founding Age stone. The gate opens at dawn without being touched.
Caelum City.hidden_truth: The city's foundations are the physical structure of the original Luminal Core apparatus. Every building in Caelum City is built on top of the most important site in the world.

The Outer Districts.description: Where Kairo and Ren live. Poorer, less Guild-monitored, more self-reliant. Ancient Founding Age structures here are used as storage, sleeping quarters, market stalls — repurposed without understanding.
The Outer Districts.hidden_truth: The outer districts sit over the densest concentration of Founding Age infrastructure. The people who live here are unconsciously affected by it — slightly better intuition, slightly faster healing, slightly stronger Resonance presence without training.

The Fractured Wastes.description: A vast desert east of Caelum where time moves incorrectly. Clocks stop. People report seeing events from other times playing out like echoes. Avoided by everyone.
The Fractured Wastes.hidden_truth: The geographic epicentre of the Arion Fracture. The Wastes are larger than any map shows — caravans that enter planning a three-day crossing sometimes walk for three weeks. Something inside is still active.

The Undercity.description: Tunnels beneath Caelum City, officially sealed. Unofficially used by people who need to move without being seen.
The Undercity.hidden_truth: The intact Founding Age city. Archives. Mechanisms still running after 2847 years. And a door at the deepest level with writing nobody alive can read — that opens into the Echo Timeline.

beyond_the_known_world.note: Exists but is not revealed in Season 1. The world is vastly larger than Caelum and its surrounding territories. This expands as the story earns it.
beyond_the_known_world.hint: There is a civilisation beyond the Fractured Wastes that has been watching Caelum City for two hundred years. They have Founding Age knowledge that the Veilkeepers do not know survived.

active_mysteries: Who really caused the Arion Fracture and why — and was it the right choice?
Why can Kairo see other timelines when no Resonant has done this in 500 years?
What happened to the original Arion people — did they die, transform, or become something the world still contains?
Why does the god trapped in the Fracture sometimes speak through random people who have no Resonance training?
What is written on the door in the Undercity?
What is beyond the Fractured Wastes — and why has no credible expedition ever returned to describe it?
Why do the Outer Districts produce more self-taught Resonants per capita than anywhere else in the known world?
What did the Chroniclers find three years ago that made their senior archivist disappear?
